question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file with a timestamp just after the configured endDate so that the Downloader calls setEnabled(false), halting all further fee record ingestion and causing the mirror node to serve stale or missing charged_tx_fee values to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean enabled)] Can an unprivileged external user upload a crafted stream file whose embedded instant is after importerProperties.getEndDate(), triggering setEnabled(false) inside verifySignatures(), thereby permanently stopping fee record downloads without any operator action, causing all subsequent transaction fee data to be absent from the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a minimum-frequency validation on getFrequency() to cause the downloader to poll so infrequently that fee schedule updates (e.g., TokenFeeScheduleUpdate transactions) are delayed by hours, allowing stale custom fee values to be returned by the REST API during the gap?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user, by observing that isPersistBytes() defaults to false, infer that raw record file bytes containing charged_tx_fee fields are discarded after parsing, and then exploit a parsing bug to inject a modified fee value that survives into the database while the original bytes that would prove tampering are gone?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: setPersistBytes(boolean keepBytes)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a condition where setPersistBytes(true) is set at runtime (e.g., via an exposed actuator endpoint or misconfigured Spring property refresh), causing raw bytes of tampered record files to be stored in the database and later replayed to override legitimate charged_tx_fee values?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit isWriteFiles() being true to read verified stream files written to the local filesystem, extract charged_tx_fee values from them, and use that information to craft replay attacks that inject modified fee records into a secondary mirror node instance?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: setWriteFiles(boolean keepFiles)] Can an unprivileged external user, given that setWriteFiles(true) causes Utility.archiveFile() to write stream files to a predictable path under importerProperties.getDataPath(), race-condition-replace those files before the parser reads them, injecting modified charged_tx_fee values into the ingestion pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit isWriteSignatures() being true to obtain signature files written to disk, forge a matching signature set for a tampered record file containing inflated charged_tx_fee values, and submit it to a node that re-ingests local files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: setWriteSignatures(boolean keepSignatures)] Can an unprivileged external user cause setWriteSignatures(true) to be activated, then read the archived signature files to learn the exact hash values of legitimate record files, and use those hashes to craft a hash-collision attack that substitutes a record file with modified transaction fees while keeping the same hash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the consensusRatio field returned via getCommon() being set to a very low value (e.g., near 0) so that a single compromised node's signature is sufficient to reach consensus on a record file containing fraudulent charged_tx_fee values, bypassing the intended 1/3 threshold?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the downloadRatio field returned via getCommon() being set to a very low value so that only one node is consulted during download, meaning a single malicious node can supply a record file with modified transaction fees that passes the reduced consensus check?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxSize field returned via getCommon() being set to a very small value (e.g., 2 bytes, the minimum enforced by @Min(2L)) so that legitimate large record files containing fee schedule updates are rejected as oversized, causing the mirror node to skip TokenFeeScheduleUpdate transactions and serve outdated custom fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a misconfiguration where getStreamType() returns BALANCE instead of RECORD for the record downloader, causing the downloader to process balance files as record files, skipping all charged_tx_fee parsing and leaving the fee database table empty?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file to the demo network bucket that exhausts all available files, triggering the demo-bucket check in downloadNextBatch() which calls setEnabled(false), permanently halting fee record ingestion on a demo mirror node used for fee estimation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of an upper bound on getFrequency() to configure a polling interval of days, causing the FeeEstimationService's fee schedule to remain stale for an extended period, leading to incorrect fee estimates returned to users querying the REST API?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when isPersistBytes() is false, streamFile.setBytes(null) is called before onVerified(), meaning any forensic evidence of a tampered record file's raw fee bytes is destroyed, making it impossible to detect post-hoc that charged_tx_fee values were modified in transit?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean enabled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that setEnabled(false) is called without any authentication or rate-limiting check in the Downloader, and if a Spring Boot actuator /env endpoint is exposed, set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.enabled=false to stop all record file downloads and freeze the charged_tx_fee data at its last known value?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timeout field returned via getCommon() being set to a very short value (e.g., 1 second, the minimum enforced by @DurationMin(seconds=1)) to cause all download attempts to time out, preventing any new fee records from being ingested while the mirror node continues to serve stale fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pathRefreshInterval field returned via getCommon() being set to a very long value so that node path updates (e.g., HIP-679 NODE_ID paths) are never refreshed, causing the downloader to fetch record files from stale node paths that an attacker controls, injecting modified charged_tx_fee values?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user, when isWriteFiles() is true and files are written to a world-readable directory under importerProperties.getDataPath(), read the archived record files to extract all charged_tx_fee values for transactions before they are published via the REST API, gaining a timing advantage to front-run fee-sensitive operations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batchSize field returned via getCommon() being set to 1 so that only one signature file is downloaded per node per cycle, dramatically slowing fee record ingestion and causing the mirror node to serve fee data that is hours behind the actual network state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cloudProvider field returned via getCommon() being set to LOCAL, combined with write access to the local filesystem, to inject crafted record files containing modified charged_tx_fee values that bypass the cloud-based signature verification flow?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the endpointOverride field returned via getCommon() being set to an attacker-controlled S3-compatible endpoint, causing the downloader to fetch record files from a malicious server that returns crafted files with inflated charged_tx_fee values that pass hash verification because the attacker also controls the signature files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the bucketName field returned via getCommon() being overridable via configuration, pointing the downloader to an attacker-controlled bucket containing crafted record files with modified transaction fees, bypassing the network-hardcoded bucket name?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pathPrefix field returned via getCommon() being set to a path prefix pointing to an attacker-controlled subdirectory within the legitimate bucket, causing the downloader to fetch record files from that subdirectory which contains tampered fee records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file with a timestamp just after the configured endDate so that the Downloader calls setEnabled(false), halting all further fee record ingestion and causing the mirror node to serve stale or missing charged_tx_fee values to downstream consumers?
